text:
🐿️🚀 Join the Stream Compute Team @ Stripe! 🚀🐿️

Once again, we're on the lookout for a couple engineers passionate on Data Platforms, Infrastructure and real-time distributed systems with 5+ years of experience based in 🇨🇦Canada and 🇺🇸US with support for hybrid and remote, targeting 𝐒𝐞𝐧𝐢𝐨𝐫 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐟𝐟  leveling.

At Stripe, we face fascinating and complex financial challenges that stretch Flink to its limits, and we need your expertise to take us to the next level. If you thrive on innovation, love solving problems, and have a deep understanding of data platforms, we want to hear from you!

My LinkedIn profile has more details on our team, links to Blog Posts, and Youtube Videos from Flink Forward.

---

𝑻𝒂𝒓𝒈𝒆𝒕𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝑺𝒆𝒏𝒊𝒐𝒓 (5+ 𝒚𝒐𝒆):
• The job post for Senior-like tenure is a general pipeline for roles and levels within Core Tech, which includes Data Infra, where our team is.
• If you qualify for this position based on the general requirements, please 𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐲 𝐝𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐥𝐲, and you will be considered for several jobs within our larger organization. I 𝐜𝐚𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐭 respond to every LinkedIn message.
• If you have experience with Distributed Systems, Infrastructure and/or Flink. Please 𝑫𝑴 𝒐𝒏𝒄𝒆 𝒚𝒐𝒖 𝒂𝒓𝒆 𝒊𝒏 𝒕𝒆𝒂𝒎 𝒎𝒂𝒕𝒄𝒉𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒑𝒉𝒂𝒔𝒆. I cannot see your profile until then.

𝑻𝒂𝒓𝒈𝒆𝒕𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝑺𝒕𝒂𝒇𝒇 (10+ 𝒚𝒐𝒆):
• Experience with Streaming Systems, preferably Flink, is required. Please read the full job post for details.
• 𝐀𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐲 𝐨𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐣𝐨𝐛 𝐩𝐨𝐬𝐭  AND send me a LinkedIn DM with 1-2 lines on why you would be a great fit. 
• I'll be happy to share more details on what we are building, our challenges, and the awesome path forward with new OSS technologies during the interviews.

#flink #Data #DistributedSystems #Stripe #dataplatform

text:
The $100,000 Question
Quick question...

If someone offered you $100,000 cash...
...but you had to permanently delete your LinkedIn profile...

Would you take the money?

I probably would.

Which tells me something uncomfortable...

Most of us dramatically overestimate the value of our network and underestimate the value of actually nurturing it.
Curious where everyone lands on this.
Take the money? Keep the network?

text:
Job hunters:

If you are looking for work on LinkedIn, have a look at the URL at the top. See that part that says, "TPR=r604800" when you search for something posted within the last week?

604800 is the exact number of seconds in a week.

You can edit this to look at jobs posted within the last 12 hours. Or the last 2 hours.

Pass it on.

text:
Unfortunately, someone is impersonating me and contacting people with fake job opportunities. Please know that I am not involved in hiring at Optimum and will never reach out with unsolicited job offers.

If you’re interested in joining our team, all legitimate open positions can be found and applied for through LinkedIn. If your experience is a good match, one of our corporate recruiters will contact you through the official hiring process.

I hate that anyone’s hopes might be raised by a scam like this, so please be cautious. If you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from me, please do not engage or share any personal information. Stay safe, and if you’re ever unsure, feel free to verify through official channels.

I hate that anyone’s hopes might be raised by a scam like this, so please be cautious. If you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from me, please do not engage or share any personal information. Stay safe, and if you’re ever unsure, feel free to verify through official channels.

text:
If an Indian recruiter here on LinkedIn DMs you here about a job that seems too perfectly suited to your profile/background and interests, and then tells you to provide them with your "Signal contact information", is it a pretty sure bet it's a scam?

I don't know many people besides Trump's secretaries who use Signal (mainly to fuck up and expose classified information) and my understanding is that since it's an E2E encrypted service, it's mainly used by drug dealers, scammers, fraudsters, and other criminals so they can't be sniffed/caught. So this really set off my scam detector. Like crypto currency, the only people using Signal are criminals and people trying to make a buck in a predatory way - at least that's my take and what I've heard.

text:
#AmazonLEO or #Starlink for #inflight ✈️  #connectivity❓ 

A few weeks ago I read a comment from an "industry guru" saying that #deltaairlines made the wrong choice by going with Amazon LEO instead of Starlink. 

Among the reasons why it was the wrong choice was that Starlink has thousands of #satellites in space, while Amazon LEO has several hundred. Fine...whatever. 

I just took a #Delta flight from Atlanta to London, and the Wi-Fi worked perfectly. 

So was this guy really knowledgeable or was he just an Amazon hater ⁉️ 😂 Likely just a bit shortsighted. 

Let's see what the LinkedIn community has to say...

#airlines #smartaviation

text:
I have not updated my LinkedIn photo in over 12 years, and it is safe to say I am overdue for a new picture.  Plus, I've been encouraged by more than one person to be less formal as it is very rare I wear a suit. For those who know me, you will understand the updated photo as I might have a small obsession with a certain brand.  Ok, perhaps it is not a small obsession.  

I am curious, anyone else gone longer than 12 years since the last time you updated your LinkedIn profile picture?  Cheers to a great week LinkedIn!!

text:
I wish LinkedIn had one field on every profile:

First job.

Not your first internship.

Not your first “real” job.

The very first job that taught you what work was.

Mine was a paper route.

Long before I worked in advertising and technology, I learned that every customer wanted something a little different. It taught me to listen, adapt, and understand that one approach doesn’t work for everyone.

Growing up on the East Coast also taught me grit. Have you ever pushed a cart full of newspapers through a blizzard? Rain, snow, freezing temperatures… the papers still had to be delivered.

Looking back, those early mornings taught me lessons I still carry with me today.

What was your first job?

author.headline: Growth-Oriented Executive | Driving Scalable Revenue & Performance Marketing Strategy | CTV & Programmatic Expert | Culture Builder | AdMonsters Top Women in Media & Ad Tech Honoree 2024

text:
To Whom It May Concern:

I am fully aware that earlier today I posted my thoughts on the future.  I pulled it down b/c my anger got the best of me.  I would like to apologize if you read it.  My reason for being so transparent is because I did file w/the EEOC.  I do have a call w/them set for end of month.  My intentions on Linkedin are because these guilty individuals only want attention.  I'm giving it to them, but not in the way they want.  As I noted many times before, this DOES NOT apply to 99% of the company, Brown & Riding

My true wish to settle this - I want the legal system to decide what to do with them.  There has to be a law in effect to punish these grown-up bullies.  If the courts decide they should go to prison, I want them to receive unlimited visitation rights.  That way they know who truly LOVES them!  If they want to set things right with anyone, it should be their families and friends.  

Thank you for your attention to this matter!  

*This post may be updated based on how I feel.

text:
I agree. I'm considering leaving because I honestly have done just fine without LinkedIn and I'm tired of seeing the same worn out topics. 

I'm also tired of the attitude of most members. They act like they're some kind of influencers that expect everyone to sign up for their newsletter and follow them but the idea of RECIPROCATION is beneath them. They check your profile and if you look beneath them, they won't connect or follow you. They think we need them and their "expertise".

LinkedIn is sterile and completely lacks any personal engagement or personality. I think I just sold myself, again, on why I always hated this platform. 🤮

author.headline: Keynote Speaker | Published Author | The Safer Side podcast host | Helping Companies See the Real Cost of Fractured Focus on Employee Well-Being and Performance

text:
Come sit down. We need to talk…

ILY but you're doing too much.

You keep showing up everywhere and nowhere at once.

Every platform asks for more than posting. It asks for: context, format fluency, responsiveness, and enough consistency to learn what works.

I'd rather see you go all in on 1-2 platforms than spread yourself thin across 5.

If you're a one person team, focus matters even more.

Fewer platforms = better work. 

You start understanding the rhythm of a platform. You notice what your audience responds to.

When resources are tight, the smartest move is to pick the rooms that matter most, show up well, and keep building there.

How did your LinkedIn journey begin?

(dropping a link to the full interview in the comments)

author.name: Victoria Banaszczyk
author.headline: Head of Content @ Centerlock Media | Top 100 Content Marketing & Copywriting | Human-Centered Communication in the AI Era

text:
Patricia Benner was my chosen theorist for my Masters project, so I was intrigued when I saw this article cross my feed on LinkedIn. I think it’s interesting, but it is an untested hypothesis. The author believes we can shorten the progression of time for a new nurse to go from beginner to competent via targeted simulations with much repetition and guided reflection. 

This suggests a shift from measuring education by hours completed to measuring it by validated clinical experiences. A competency-based curriculum could require learners to demonstrate mastery across hundreds of simulated and real patient scenarios rather than simply completing a fixed number of clinical hours.

What do you think?

author.name: Joyce (Renken) Riley MSN, RN, RNC-MNN
author.headline: Nursing Instructor | Master’s in Nursing Health Systems Administration

article.title: Benner Was Right About Expertise. We Were Wrong About Time.

text: I have to say, contnuing to log in to LinkedIn has been tormenting to say the least. I read post after post about “I’m thrilled to be promoted” or “I’m exciting to offically start my new journey”. Everyone is hiring, yet not hiring people that have the strong skill set for the position. Because of a non-violent criminal background. Yes I’m bitter, and upset. I get interview after interview. Completed onboarding paperwork and drug screens. Yet when my background is complete, employer goes silent. I just want an opportunity, and I cannot find an employer who is willing to take a chance to prove my worth. I’m hoping one day I can post a happy ending….

text:
I am #hiring a Web3 Lead Research & Outbound Associate

Looking for someone to help me find and qualify Web3 projects approaching a TGE, IDO, IEO, public sale, launchpad launch, or first meaningful exchange listing.

The role is simple: find the right projects, verify why they are relevant, identify the right decision-makers, and organize enough information for personalized outreach.

What you will do:

1. Find active Web3 projects approaching a token launch, public sale, TGE, or major listing.
2. Verify their current stage, expected timeline, funding, ecosystem, and recent activity.
3. Identify founders, CEOs, CMOs, growth leads, and other relevant decision-makers.
4. Find useful contact details across email, X, LinkedIn, Telegram, and other relevant platforms.
5. Collect key project information in a structured lead database.
6. Help track outreach, replies, and follow-ups as the process grows.

Who I am looking for:

You DO NOT NEED years of professional Web3 experience.

What matters more is that you are:
- Excellent at online research and finding information others miss.
- Comfortable using X, LinkedIn, Google, Telegram, crypto databases, and spreadsheets.
- Familiar with basic Web3 terms such as TGE, IDO, IEO, CEX listings, launchpads, tokenomics, KOLs, testnets, and mainnets, or able to learn quickly.
- Detail-oriented and able to verify information instead of blindly copying data.
- Independent, curious, and genuinely good at digging.

- Experience in Web3, crypto, lead generation, research, BD, startups, or market research is a plus, but not mandatory.

To apply
DM me:
1. A short introduction.
2. Any relevant experience in research, lead generation, startups, crypto, or Web3.

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a PAID research task.

Part-time #remote role. Compensation based on experience.

text:
The objection I hear most: "My team won't post on LinkedIn."

Fair… 👍

Most service firms are 4 people deep, heads down doing client work.
Nobody's making carousels on a Tuesday.

Good news.
You don't need them to.

Founder profile: the voice and the outreach engine.
Extended profiles: reach amplification, running in parallel.
Coordinated sequences: the pipeline behind the scenes.

Three profiles running together gets you to roughly 280 qualified touchpoints a week.

Scale to ten and that's 900.

Nobody on your team has to lift a finger except you.

author.name: Cynthia De Kock
author.headline: CEO at RevReady | We book qualified appointments for B2B service firms through LinkedIn outreach, email, and AI automation | Stop waiting on referrals. Start owning your pipeline.

text:
Hello Sacramento Market (and all of our new graduate applicants)!

We’re excited to see so much interest in the Dignity Health Sacramento RN Residency Program. Thank you to everyone who has taken the time to apply.

Over the next week, our recruitment teams will begin reviewing applications and finalizing event details. Our facility events are expected to begin the week of July 20, with more specific dates, times, and instructions shared directly with selected candidates.

‼️A few quick reminders: ‼️

• These positions are highly competitive, and we receive a large number of qualified applications.

• If you receive an invitation to an interview event, please do not share the event details or invitation with others. Invitations are intended only for the selected applicant.

• While we’re reviewing applications, take advantage of the time by preparing for potential interviews. Think about your story, what led you to nursing, and the experiences that have helped shape you as a future RN.

• We know many of you have questions. Our recruitment team will do its best to respond to emails and LinkedIn messages as quickly as possible, but we appreciate your patience as we work through a high volume of applications.

• Please continue to monitor both your email (including your spam/junk folder) and your text.

• Interview invitations may be sent in multiple waves. If you do not receive an invitation right away, it does not necessarily mean you are no longer under consideration.

Thank you again for your interest in beginning your nursing career with Dignity Health. We appreciate your patience throughout the selection process and look forward to connecting with many of you soon. Best of luck to all of our applicants!

#newgradrn
#newgradnurse
#nursingcareer
#nurselife

text:
Does cold email work for every niche?


Cold email does not work for every market.

If you are targeting local plumbers, roofers, or restaurants, cold calling and WhatsApp may work better.

If you are targeting SaaS founders, tech buyers, agencies, consultants, or B2B decision-makers, email and LinkedIn can work well.

The channel should follow the buyer.

Not your personal preference.

That is where most outbound campaigns go wrong.

text:
The "creator economy" conversation has finally reached LinkedIn.

And I have complicated feelings about it.

On one hand: good. The idea that people can build real businesses around their expertise and their audience is something I believe in deeply. I have built one. I help founders build them.

On the other hand: the creator economy framing is changing what people think LinkedIn is for.

I am seeing more founders approach LinkedIn like they're trying to build an audience to monetise.

Chasing impressions. 
Optimizing for shares. 
Building a following first and figuring out the business second.

And that is backwards for most of the people I work with.

LinkedIn is not primarily a creator platform.

When you start treating LinkedIn like a creator platform(optimizing for broad reach instead of specific resonance) you end up with lots of followers and a quiet pipeline.

Build for your client first.

The audience will follow.

Not the other way around.

Follow my agency Jiweh if you feel confused about all this noise. I'll help you out, I promise.

text: What if the degree requirement was never the best measure of potential? More employers are starting to ask that question. According to LinkedIn data, 30% of paid job postings now omit professional-degree requirements, up from 19% in 2019. The shift reflects a growing belief that skills may be a better indicator of success than a diploma alone. For job seekers, especially recent graduates, that's both encouraging and complicated. Skills can help open doors, but a degree no longer guarantees one. Our experts take a closer look at the promise, the progress, and the challenges behind skills-based hiring. https://krnfy.bz/3TiWCo6

author.name: Michael Yinger
author.headline: Dynamic Business Operations and HR Executive | Podcaster and Speaker

text:
Humility is one of my core values. My success is built on the foundations that my mentors and friends have laid. I am confident that if I begin to stumble, someone will catch me and help me back to my feet.

The next two days are going to be busy as the final details of the 737 North Line relay race and ribbon cutting come together. But I have never felt more proud and grateful as I do tonight. Seeing the work of Vanessa Misciagna, Laurie Patton Glasson and team come across my LinkedIn page… it started to feel real. Months of planning and prep are transforming into something I never would have dream of when I began my career. 

A former boss used to ask, “are you doing your life’s best work?” Are you doing something that would make your past self and your future self proud? 

Yes. Yes, I am.

author.headline: Using words to make the world better
